entries.source:
Date: August 22, 1968
Hello. i am writing this log to keep myself sane. I couldnt find a way to do the most basic thing on this old janky butt computer so I had Desmond add it in, the log feature. til now ive just been adding my own notes to the bestiary whatever i know. My brother said i should keep a journal so im doing what i can! I am writing in a proffessional manner, as these logs could become a historical record, as they will projbably be here for years, just as long as there isnt a clean wipe. that is what desmond said


If you are reading it in the future, you are probably from a nother crew. The turnover rate here is enormous, maybe cause this job sucks and every one turns over dead! Maybe I can give some help when I have some expirience. End log.
Oh, our names are: Sigurd (me), Richard, Desmond, Jess.
entries.target:
日期：1968 年 8 月 22 日
你好。我写下这篇日志，是为了让自己保持清醒。我连这台老旧破电脑上最基本的功能都找不到，所以让 Desmond 把日志功能加了进去。在这之前，我一直只是把自己知道的东西随手写进生物图鉴里。我哥说我应该写日记，所以我只能尽力！我会用一种专业的方式来写，因为这些日志可能会成为历史记录，反正只要没有被彻底清空，它们大概会在这里待上很多年。Desmond 是这么说的。


如果未来有人读到它，你大概来自另一支船员队伍。这里的人员流动率大得离谱，也许是因为这工作烂透了，而且每个人最后都流动成死人！等我有了经验，也许能帮上一点忙。日志结束。
哦，我们的名字是：Sigurd（我）、Richard、Desmond、Jess。

这套衣服把我勒得像该死的木乃伊！我是个成年人，裤裆那里给点空间行不行！它要勒死我了，我快死了！我终于找到一份工作，结果真和老爸总说的一样。这里每个人都臭。尤其是 Rich。我要把他扔进湖里，我才不管那湖是什么做的。我喜欢值监视器班。哦
我忘了日期，是 1968 年 8 月 24 日。这个键盘打出来 dsadjilfasqt..f/g；


今天我们找到了几口煎锅和一根大钉子。几乎不值钱。公司到底拿这些东西干什么用

entries.source:
we got in the "swing of things" the past few days. thats what Rich keeps saying, he smells like a rancid tuna can. worst summer camp I've ever been o n. oh.. Date is August 27, 1968. \r
we make sure someone always has a shovel for bashing anything that moves, and we always put someone on "screen duty" so they can open these big security doors with letters and numbers. ask Desmond the wizard how that works, I think he just types in the code thats on the door. Thats it\r
\r
we sold some goods to the company today. i got m y nerves chilled listening to the WACKO soundfs behind the counter. No one else gave any. ?? \r
My flashlight didn't even go back there, the beam just went dark.\r
\r
entries.target:
这几天我们算是“上手了”。Rich 一直这么说，他闻起来像一罐腐烂的金枪鱼。这是我参加过最糟的夏令营。哦……日期是 1968 年 8 月 27 日。
我们会确保总有人拿着铲子，好把任何会动的东西敲烂，也总会安排一个人值“监视器班”，这样就能打开那些带字母和数字的大安全门。问问 Desmond 那个巫师这是怎么运作的，我觉得他只是把门上的代码输进去。就这样。

今天我们把一些货卖给了公司。听着柜台后面那些疯疯癫癫的声音，我浑身发冷。其他人一点反应都没有。？？
我的手电光甚至照不到那后面，光束直接变黑了。

entries.source:
date is August 31, 1968. i got camera duty again, i hate walking on this moon anyway. ahah jokes on them it, just started raining!!! Im just sitting. cant sleep on these tiny beds for ants


Ive been thinking a lot. i think this job is shady. If youre reading this you probbably got here the same as me. the pay is good, the contract only lasts a season. they ran you through their "assessmennt exam" on the phone with a weird voice. you signed the contract on the shuttle with the rest of your crew. But the whole time you didnt talk to anyone. The shuttle was autopiloted, I think the voice on the phone was fake.  I;ve had the wrost dreams, I think   I just want to go back home. but im not gonna crawl home, crying and scratching on dad's front door. thats not his son. thats not Sigurd!
entries.target:
日期是 1968 年 8 月 31 日。我又值摄像头班了，反正我也讨厌在这个卫星上走路。哈哈，笑话在他们身上，刚好开始下雨了！！！我就坐着。这些给蚂蚁睡的小床根本睡不着。


我想了很多。我觉得这份工作很可疑。如果你正在读这个，你大概和我一样来到这里。工资不错，合同只持续一个季度。他们用电话里那个怪声音让你做“评估考试”。你和其他船员一起在穿梭机上签了合同。但整个过程中你没有和任何人说过话。穿梭机是自动驾驶的，我觉得电话里的声音是假的。我做了最糟糕的梦，我想我只是想回家。但我不会哭着爬回家，在老爸门口抓门。那不是他的儿子。那不是 Sigurd！

entries.source:
i got woken up early this morning because we were going to the company building to sell our useless garbage. The rate was 120% which Desmond says is rare and he didnt want to miss it, hes been watching it like the stupid stocks. 

it still sucks here, Rich still smells like a piece of hsit .i can barely sleep. i heard the wrorst sound behind the wall of the company building, it sounded like the pestle and bowl my mom crushed up her seeds and spices in. i still hear it. no one else heard it. Jess thinks i;m "home sick," no im sick of this cheap suit\r
date: Septermber 4, 1968
entries.target:
今天一大早我就被叫醒，因为我们要去公司大楼卖掉那些没用的垃圾。回收率是 120%，Desmond 说这很少见，他不想错过，他盯着那东西就像盯着愚蠢的股票。

这里还是烂透了，Rich 还是闻起来像一坨屎。我几乎睡不着。我听见公司大楼墙后传来最糟糕的声音，像我妈妈用研钵和杵碾碎种子和香料的声音。我现在还听得见。其他人都没听见。Jess 觉得我是“想家”，不，我只是受够了这套廉价制服。
日期：1968 年 9 月 4 日

entries.source:
they had me on screen duty, and then they all came waddling back,, but Rich wasn't there, and they said they couldnt find him. Desmond said it turned his neck but he didnt see it. I wanted to find Rich even if he was dead, but they are cowards! their faces are blank like idiots, theres nothing moving in their stupid skulls, all they wanted to do is leave. they were going to leave me too if i went out. we all hated Rich but we didnt want this. this isnt worth it, its just not worth it. we got a pair of scissors, a box full of stamps and a bundle of cords to sell for nothing. its not wort h it, what does the company even want it for?


the date we last saw Rich is September 7, 1968
entries.target:
他们让我值监视器班，然后他们全都摇摇晃晃地回来了，可 Rich 不在。他们说找不到他。Desmond 说那东西转过了脖子，但他没看见。我想去找 Rich，哪怕他死了也要找，可他们都是懦夫！他们脸上一片空白，像白痴一样，愚蠢的脑壳里什么都没动，他们只想离开。如果我出去，他们也会把我丢下。我们都讨厌 Rich，但我们不想这样。这不值得，真的不值得。我们找到一把剪刀、一盒邮票和一捆线，卖不了几个钱。不值得，公司到底要这些东西干什么？


我们最后一次见到 Rich 的日期是 1968 年 9 月 7 日

entries.source:
date: September 13, 1968

for some reason i was the one who had to call the company number to report the acciddent. they were too scared to do it. It was the same fake voice i remember from the interview on the phone at home and the stupid training videos. but it heard what i said i think, cause it said they would contact the family and find a replacement and something e lse. It talked so fast.


I learned you can hear the screams all the time in the walkie talkie when you;re  at the wall of the Company building. they didnt believe me, well now they do. It sounds just like when i heard it a couple weeks ago. They want to quit. I said I'm not a coward. I just got this job
entries.target:
日期：1968 年 9 月 13 日

不知道为什么，最后是我打电话给公司号码报告事故。他们太害怕，不敢打。那是我记得的同一个假声音，就是在家里电话面试和愚蠢培训录像里的那个。但我觉得它听懂了我说的话，因为它说他们会联系家属，找替补，还有别的什么。它说得太快了。


我发现，当你在公司大楼那面墙旁边时，可以一直从对讲机里听到尖叫声。他们不相信我，好吧，现在他们信了。那声音就像我几周前听到的一样。他们想辞职。我说我不是懦夫。我才刚得到这份工作。

entries.source:
I talked ot a voice on the walkie talkie, it was like a part of the pestle and morter. he told me the Golden planet actually existed, its not a legend. and he told me it didn't just hit a meteor. He said the planet was swallowed up by the "beast", and they were in its body being diggested. i asked what the beast is, and he said he didnt know!. he said it ate the planet and they forgot everything. \r
\r
i couldnt get him to stop talking. but i said he was on the other side of a big wall and i could get him out. i said he was inside the building. and thats when he started freaking out/. i couldn't make out a word, I think he said something about "spitting out the rinds" So i just turned it off. what a wack
\r
Jess says the golden planet is just a story. I said  I KNOW, I'm not an idiot. Well she said i should quit, and she quits if i do. so shes staying
entries.target:
我在对讲机里和一个声音说话，它像是研钵和杵的一部分。他告诉我黄金星球真的存在，不是什么传说。他还告诉我它不是被陨石撞了。他说那颗星球被“野兽”吞掉了，他们正在它的身体里被消化。我问那野兽是什么，他说他不知道！他说它吃掉了那颗星球，而他们忘记了一切。

我没法让他停下来说话。但我说他在一堵大墙的另一边，我可以把他救出来。我说他在那栋建筑里面。然后他就开始吓坏了。我听不清一个字，我觉得他说了什么“把果皮吐出来”。所以我就把它关了。真是个怪人。

Jess 说黄金星球只是个故事。我说我知道，我又不是白痴。然后她说我应该辞职，如果我辞她也辞。所以她会留下来。

entries.source:
date: September 18, 1968
the past weekend it feels like Desmond and Jess woke up. we;re supposed to get our fourth member of the crew in a few days. We arent taking any risks anymore, we dont get as much but it feels safer, and ive gotten better sleep. but its still ass. i can still smell Rich i swear to gosh he smelled that bad


i was talking about how the voice on the phone sounded fake. Then Desmond got this weird idea. he's the wizard and all so he thinks theres a way to use the phone call i made to the company number to find ourt where the voice comes from. i dont know why hed want to do that but he seems serious. He;s been using the terminal a lot.
entries.target:
日期：1968 年 9 月 18 日
过去这个周末，感觉 Desmond 和 Jess 终于醒过来了。我们几天后应该会迎来第四名船员。我们不再冒任何风险，赚得没那么多，但感觉安全些了，我也睡得好了一点。但还是很烂。我发誓我还能闻到 Rich，他真的臭到那种程度。


我当时在说电话里的声音听起来很假。然后 Desmond 有了个怪点子。他毕竟是巫师什么的，所以他觉得有办法用我打给公司号码的那通电话，找出那个声音来自哪里。我不知道他为什么想这么做，但他看起来很认真。他最近一直在用终端。

entries.source:
Barber

I had a dream about the Barber
One day he made me a wig
Because his scissors were too big
entries.target:
理发师

我梦见了理发师
有一天他给我做了一顶假发
因为他的剪刀太大了

entries.source:
The guardians of the house. 

They watch with one tireless eye, which only senses movement. It remembers the last creature it saw, whether they are moving or not.
entries.target:
屋宅的守卫。

它们用一只不知疲倦的眼睛注视着，那只眼睛只感知运动。它会记住自己上一次看到的生物，无论它们是否还在移动。

面具黄蜂

面具黄蜂

entries.source: A large mammal of the family
entries.target: 一种属于该科的大型哺乳动物

entries.source: They are social, hunting in very large packs.
entries.target: 它们具有社会性，会以大型群体狩猎。

entries.source: They pose little threat and have a generally passive temperament towards humans
entries.target: 它们威胁很小，通常对人类表现被动

entries.source: A very large arthropod of the class chilopoda.
entries.target: 一种非常巨大的唇足纲节肢动物。

entries.source: Its exoskeleton is somewhat fragile, and they can die from long falls.
entries.target: 它的外骨骼相当脆弱，长距离坠落可能致死。

entries.source: They are relentless hunters, typically at the top of their food chain.
entries.target: 它们是执着的猎手，通常处于食物链顶端。

entries.source: Their main weaknesses are their intelligence and complete lack of hearing.
entries.target: 它们的主要弱点是智力低下以及完全没有听觉。

entries.source: If you come across a thumper, your best means of survival are leaving its line of sight
entries.target: 如果遇到重锤兽，最好的生存方式是离开它的视线

entries.source: Their behaviour is stubborn and fearless to a fault.
entries.target: 它们的行为固执且近乎鲁莽地无所畏惧。

entries.source: Their planet of origin is unknown
Due to this and their other harmful effects on many ecosystems and agriculture
entries.target: 由于这一点以及它们对许多生态系统和农业造成的其他危害

entries.source: Any sighting of Cadaver Growths must be reported to the ITDA.
entries.target: 任何尸体生长体目击事件都必须上报 ITDA。

entries.source: Do not enter a room where Cadaver Growths are present.
entries.target: 不要进入存在尸体生长体的房间。

entries.source: Please have a good night, and work hard.
entries.target: 祝你夜晚平安，并努力工作。

entries.source:
September 27, 1968
desmond wants me to writ e down what we figured out and keep my "nonsense to a minimum". So im going to put SO MUCH of my non sense. screw him. The new crew member is named Lucas and he is so confused and scared a ll the time, what a baby, at least he doesnt smell like a monster. its been so sunny even though its september, and these suits dont take it well
ok so desmond "traced the call" and found out theres a group of people somewhere else that pretends to call us from the company building. they are across the whole solar system !! why would they be so far away.


Desmond says he doesnt know, but i think. What if there really is a big monster in the company building like the voice told me on the wlakie talkie? They trapped it, and we feed it to keep it tame.
entries.target:
1968 年 9 月 27 日
Desmond 想让我把我们弄明白的东西写下来，并且把我的“废话控制到最低”。所以我要写超级多的废话。去他的。新来的船员叫 Lucas，他一直又困惑又害怕，真是个宝宝，至少他闻起来不像怪物。虽然已经九月了，但天气一直特别晴，这些制服可受不了。
好吧，Desmond “追踪了那通电话”，发现别的地方有一群人假装从公司大楼给我们打电话。他们隔着整个太阳系！！为什么他们会那么远。


Desmond 说他不知道，但我在想。如果公司大楼里真的有个大怪物，就像对讲机里的声音告诉我的那样呢？他们困住了它，而我们喂养它，让它保持温顺。
entries.note: TerminalNode LogFile10 第二批长文/星球全文精翻。

entries.source:
September 30, 1968


i keep having bad dreams about the Company building where the Company breaks out of that giant concrete wall. But i dont know if it looks like anything, i just think its really fast, and its so big it covers the sky. We cant figure out a way to know if the voices on the phone are real, desmond says its too far away. i asked him if he could ttake control of the autopilot and he said im crazy and that could kill us. yes i am crazy desmond


he says we would have to get our own private flight to go to the cordinates, and who knows what would actually be there. So, what if its nothing?
i started wondering how i cant remember how we really got here, no I just remember little things like the shuttle flight to the building where we signed our contracts. But i dont remember getting on the shuttle, i dont even remember saying bye to dad. sometmies i feel this deep pit in my stomache and i have to think just to remember my name. in my dreams it feels like the company isnt trapped in there at all, its just hiding. I dont know if were going home
entries.target:
1968 年 9 月 30 日


我一直做关于公司大楼的噩梦，梦见公司从那面巨大的混凝土墙里冲出来。但我不知道它看起来像什么，我只觉得它特别快，而且大到遮住天空。我们没法知道电话里的声音到底是不是真的，Desmond 说距离太远了。我问他能不能接管自动驾驶，他说我疯了，而且那样会害死我们。是啊 Desmond，我就是疯了。


他说我们必须自己包一趟私人航班去那些坐标，谁知道那里到底会有什么。所以，如果那里什么都没有呢？
我开始想，为什么我记不得自己到底是怎么来到这里的。不，我只记得一点点，比如去签合同那栋楼的穿梭机航程。但我不记得自己登上穿梭机，也不记得和老爸道别。有时候我胃里会有一个很深的坑，我得努力想才记得自己的名字。在梦里，公司感觉根本没有被困在里面，它只是在躲着。我不知道我们还能不能回家。
entries.note: TerminalNode LogFile11 第二批长文/星球全文精翻。

entries.source:
date : October 1st, 1968
were out of snacks and lemonade packets again. desmond thinks stun grenades are "more benefficial and economic" well if he puts off ordering my stuff for another day, im going to take a crap on the control lever and ask him what he thinks about his stun grenades then. desmond dont read this

i know im not the wizard but I guess im the computer guy again today. every time i go outside on vow they say i get pale like im dead, so they put me on screen duty. they think i got soft or something. whatever, Mr. Doofus Lucas is the scared one. good luck keeping him from losing his freaking mind. I could stroll out at night like its my back yard!! Its just that i saw Rich on a hill. he was over on the orther side of the river. but that was three days ago. Im not scared, its just getting cold outside. i think they jus twant me to have screen duty every day now, and they all got an excuse

i miss dad. i hope he isnt staying on titan. people are saying its not going to look the same in two years. jess told us they a re about to go to war and everyone is just waiting for it. every time we go to sell, the company building is shaking like theres a loud furnace inside. we can barely sleep to meet the quota, and it gets worse every time. God, i feel like im being squeezed through a needle. i wish we could go back. it was better working for Dad even when i got nothing, just daily alowance. i liked when he would drive us out of town to see the waterfall and we walked up those old wooden stairs. I just wanted a real job
entries.target:
日期：1968 年 10 月 1 日
零食和柠檬水粉又没了。Desmond 觉得震撼弹“更有益也更经济”。好吧，如果他再拖一天不订我的东西，我就要在控制杆上拉屎，然后问他怎么看他的震撼弹。Desmond 别读这个。

我知道我不是巫师，但我猜今天我又是电脑男了。每次我出去 Vow，他们都说我白得像死人，所以让我值监视器班。他们觉得我变软弱了还是什么。随便吧，胆小的是傻瓜 Lucas 先生。祝你们好运，别让他疯掉。我晚上出去散步就像走在自家后院！！只是我在山坡上看见了 Rich。他在河的另一边。但那是三天前的事。我不害怕，只是外面开始冷了。我觉得他们现在就是想让我每天值监视器班，而且每个人都有借口。

我想老爸。我希望他没有留在 Titan。大家都在说那里两年后会完全变样。Jess 告诉我们他们快要打仗了，每个人都只是在等。每次我们去卖东西，公司大楼都在震，像里面有一座轰鸣的熔炉。为了完成指标，我们几乎睡不了觉，而且每次都更糟。天啊，我感觉自己像被挤过针眼。我希望我们能回去。以前给老爸干活还好些，哪怕我什么都拿不到，只有每日零用钱。我喜欢他开车带我们出城去看瀑布，然后我们走上那些旧木楼梯。我只是想要一份真正的工作。
entries.note: TerminalNode LogFile12 第二批长文/星球全文精翻。

entries.source:
Desmond. October 7, 1968. Using the log entry system I designed for Sigurd, but I'm making this one private to keep it safe from discovery, as it would damage the team morale. We might not meet our quota tonight.

I believe it's all a guise. We're supposed to think it's all just a transaction, but our real job is feeding ourselves into the machine. How long until its fullness ends and its hunger is insatiable? God knows, maybe it has to do with all these desolate moons. Whoever reads this, I am sorry to burden you with this. Please have a good night, and work hard. As what else is there for us to do?
entries.target:
Desmond。1968 年 10 月 7 日。我正在使用我为 Sigurd 设计的日志系统，但我会把这一篇设为私人，以免被发现，因为它会打击团队士气。今晚我们可能完不成指标。

我相信这一切都是伪装。我们应该以为这只是一场交易，但我们真正的工作，是把自己喂进那台机器。还要多久它的饱足才会结束，而饥饿变得无法满足？天知道，也许这和这些荒凉的卫星有关。无论是谁读到这篇，我很抱歉把这些负担交给你。愿你今夜平安，并努力工作。除此之外，我们还能做什么呢？
entries.note: TerminalNode LogFile13 第二批长文/星球全文精翻。

entries.source:
October 10th 1968


I havent felt like writing. no time. but last night I swear i heard dad talking to me from the vent, the one under the charger. i dont feel crazy. Man, thats what they all say, yea yea i know! Well, im writing this log to keep myself sane, right? so ill write about it. he was asking where he was, breathing really deep like he couldnt breath, like he was getting pulled into the engine. he sounded scared. he said he was thirsty


i never heard dad like that. maybe i just dont get enugh sleep. jeesh, how am i gona do it now. maybe i should just open the thing up and look.


maybe its cause Im feeling bad, cause we sold the baby bird we stole from that giant wingless one that's always stink-eyeing us on Vow. Man it could run thouggh, we had to leave on the spot, and Mr. Doofus Lucas has been chattering his teeth ever since. im not doing that again.
They made ME< put the baby bird on the desk. I think I shouldn't have done it. i know why they made me do it now.... theyre even more guilty than i am. cowards. So i went and told it to desmond's face. Do your own dirty work.

He went all dumb and and said guilty what? For selling the baby bird egg!Then, i cant belive it, he said it's no different than the eggs you eat for breakfast. i said it BROKE OUT AND LOOKED AT US, and i wouldnt eat that! it was ALIVE. And then, what he said. 1968 年 10 月 10 日


我一直不想写。没时间。但昨晚我发誓我听见老爸从通风口里跟我说话，就是充电器下面那个。我不觉得自己疯了。老天，他们都这么说，是是是我知道！好吧，我写这篇日志是为了保持清醒，对吧？那我就写。他问自己在哪，呼吸很深，像喘不过气，像被拉进引擎里。他听起来很害怕。他说他渴。


我从没听过老爸那样说话。也许我只是睡得不够。天啊，我现在该怎么办。也许我应该打开那东西看看。


也许是因为我心里难受，因为我们卖掉了从 Vow 上那只巨大的无翼家伙那里偷来的小鸟。它总是在恶狠狠地盯着我们。老天它跑得可真快，我们只能当场离开，傻瓜 Lucas 先生从那以后牙齿一直打颤。我不会再这么干了。
他们让“我”把小鸟放到桌上。我觉得我不该那么做。我现在知道他们为什么让我做了……他们比我还内疚。懦夫。所以我当面告诉 Desmond：自己的脏活自己干。

他傻住了，说内疚什么？就因为卖掉一颗小鸟蛋！然后，我简直不敢相信，他说这和你早饭吃的鸡蛋没什么不同。我说它破壳出来还看着我们，而我不会吃那个！它是活的。然后他说了那些话：“它能搬废料吗？它能帮我们达成指标吗？它能提高效率和吞吐量并增强团队协同吗？它能不能叭叭叭叭叭叭叭叭叭叭叭叭

Date: October 13, 1968
This is Desmond. Sigurd is unable to write. But I feel the need to keep a record, and none of us are sleeping tonight. After the incident, he's practically catatonic. He says he heard his father's voice in the air intake vent under the charger several days ago; I wish he'd told us before the incident, but I'm liable to believe he's being honest at least to himself. In this state he'd be a danger to himself and others, so we've been reduced to a three-man crew--a hard blow I'm afraid we can't afford.

This is how Jess and Lucas recounted it. There was a scraping sound, of which they soon located the vent as the source. They took this as a sign of the ship needing maintenance and perhaps an oil change. Lucas went to grab the tools just to check. However before he returned, the vent burst with a chunky, red fluid, with what were almost surely bits of bone and hair, which spread across the floor, like the contents of a clogged toilet. Fully emptying the vent, cleaning out the ship, and eradicating the stench was a challenge I wouldn't wish on anyone, especially with Sigurd trying to stop us. Now the smell of bleach burning our nostrils is a relief.

Why? Why did it happen? I cannot reasonably entertain Sigurd's "explanation" that somehow his father was inside the ship. He has always been quite imaginative, not to mention his current state. I believe the crew before us--or the crew before that--is simply responsible for attempting to avoid a fine, and the method was to hide their mistake. The victim's remains have been dumped out, and we will never identify them.

It doesn't matter. As I stated, this is unsustainable. I have followed every step of the handbook. I have tried to make things work. I have maximized our efficiency. I have kept our morale in check. Now, even I am wringing my hands. It's like this job is just designed to break me! I've done everything right, I've done everything I can, and still, we would have to pull a miracle just to get back on track. The only course of action left is to do what I cannot. So maybe I will. 

I will hand in my letter of resignation personally. It’s time to see who we've been working for. No, Sigurd, this is not our real job. Our work has just begun. And there is no way out but further in.

日期：1968 年 10 月 13 日
我是 Desmond。Sigurd 无法书写。但我觉得有必要留下记录，今晚我们没有一个人睡得着。事故之后，他几乎陷入紧张症状态。他说几天前在充电器下面的进气通风口里听见了父亲的声音；我希望他能在事故前告诉我们，但我倾向于相信，至少对他自己而言，他是诚实的。在这种状态下，他会对自己和他人构成危险，所以我们被迫缩减成三人船员，这恐怕是我们承受不起的沉重打击。

Jess 和 Lucas 是这样复述的。先是有刮擦声，他们很快确认声音来自通风口。他们以为这是飞船需要维护，也许该换油的迹象。Lucas 去拿工具，只是想检查一下。然而在他回来之前，通风口爆开，喷出厚重的红色液体，其中几乎可以确定混着骨头和头发碎片，像堵塞马桶里的东西一样铺满地板。彻底清空通风口、清理飞船、消除恶臭，是我不愿让任何人经历的挑战，尤其 Sigurd 还试图阻止我们。现在，漂白剂烧灼鼻孔的气味反而是一种解脱。

为什么？为什么会发生？我无法理性接受 Sigurd 的“解释”，说他的父亲不知怎么就在飞船里面。他一直很有想象力，更不用说他现在的状态。我相信我们之前的船员，或者再之前的船员，只是为了避免罚款而试图掩盖错误。他们把受害者的遗骸倒了出来，而我们永远无法确认其身份。

这不重要。正如我所说，这无法持续。我遵循了手册上的每一步。我努力让事情运转。我最大化了我们的效率。我控制了我们的士气。现在，连我也束手无策。这份工作就像是专门为了击垮我而设计的！我做对了一切，我做了我能做的一切，而即便如此，我们也必须依靠奇迹才能回到正轨。唯一剩下的行动，就是去做我不能做的事。所以，也许我会去做。

我会亲自递交辞职信。是时候看看我们一直在为谁工作了。不，Sigurd，这不是我们真正的工作。我们的工作才刚刚开始。而除了继续深入，没有出去的路。

是的，我是 Sigurd，前来报告。今天我们工作了。
日期：10 月 15 日




好吧行。他们想让我写。他们觉得我看起来很难过，以为把想法重新写进这台大机器里，我脸上就会挂起大大的笑容。我很开心，我只是想工作，这就是我想要的一切。我只想工作，什么也不说。这犯法吗？？？？不，我当然不想吃东西，因为我不饿。

他们一定真的以为我要把我们炸上天还是怎样。与此同时，他们却让 Desmond 偷懒。

他正试图搞出一场大叛乱。比如我们要去柜台那里朝它开枪吗？听起来不错 Desmond，真是太棒了。他一直说得像要罢工一样，而罢工这个词真的很酷，意思就是坐在你肮脏肥胖的屁股上，挖着鼻子，还以为自己很酷。我进来时差点踩到他的一张纸滑倒。当时我拿着一台生锈的金属研磨机，它可能会切掉什么东西。我会确保自己失血跑掉前也拿它用在他身上。抱歉，这有点恶心。

整面墙都贴满了他的图表。至少他还是那个巫师。也许他会发明一根大魔杖，把我们传送出去。然后他还觉得“我”疯了。那是老爸！我听见他，我还以为自己疯了。但那就是老爸。我看见了。是啊，我不知道他怎么会在飞船通风口里。我要吐了。这说不通。你觉得我是在编吗？
好吧，我觉得“你”才疯了，Desmond。要是我过去把你的纸全拿走撕碎会怎样。哦天啊。老爸死了。我们杀了他。

嗯……写东西没用。我还是回去工作吧。我只想工作。

----------------------

CONDITIONS: Desolate and flat with unbreathable air, Embrion's surface layer is almost entirely made of amethyst quartz, which is made possible by Embrion's distance to its nearest star and its resulting geothermal activity. The surface of Embrion is scorchingly hot.

EARTH LEVIATHAN

Sigurd's danger level: 2% cause they can't hide from the ship cmaeras!!

Scientific name: Hemibdella-gigantis
The reverently named Earth Leviathan, of the family Piscicolidae, is one of the largest invertibrates found around the Thistle Nebula. None have been captured, so not much is known of their biology. 

They seem to behave as predators. It's speculated they can burrow as far as 40 meters underground, judging by the incredible excavations they can leave behind. They can detect even the slightest vibrations, and for this reason it's not recommended to stay still if they are nearby; that is a myth. Instead, if you hear them burrowing, retrace your steps.

大地利维坦

西格德危险等级：2%，因为它们躲不过飞船摄像头！！

学名：Hemibdella-gigantis
被敬畏地称为大地利维坦的这种生物属于鱼蛭科，是蓟状星云周边发现的最大无脊椎动物之一。尚无个体被捕获，因此对其生物学了解不多。

它们似乎表现为捕食者。根据它们留下的惊人挖掘痕迹推测，它们能在地下深达 40 米处掘进。它们可以感知哪怕最细微的振动，因此如果它们在附近，并不建议你站着不动；那是个传言。相反，如果你听见它们在地下掘进，请沿原路返回。

entries.source:
EYELESS DOG

Scientific name: Leo caecus
A large mammal of the family Saeptivus. They are social, hunting in very large packs. They have also been called "breathing lions" for their recognizable sound and large mouths. They are endurance hunters and attempt to make up for their lack of sight with their sense of hearing. It's a popular myth that they often mistake the sounds of their own kind for prey, entering fights within their own packs.

Their behaviour is unique from other pack animals in their tendency to spread out far to cover distance. When an eyeless dog has found prey, it roars to alert others in the near vicinity, who will also sound the alarm, sometimes resulting in a kind of chain reaction. Eyeless dogs can be dangerous in swarms. However, they are characteristically clumsy, taking guesses at their prey's exact location which are often incorrect.
entries.target:

学名：Leo caecus
一种属于 Saeptivus 科的大型哺乳动物。它们具有社会性，会以巨大群体狩猎。由于其独特的声音和巨大的嘴，它们也被称为“呼吸狮”。它们是耐力型猎手，并试图用听觉弥补视觉缺失。一个流行传言认为，它们经常把同类的声音误认成猎物，从而在群体内部打斗。

它们的行为不同于其他群居动物，倾向于分散得很远以覆盖距离。当一只无眼犬发现猎物时，它会咆哮以警告附近其他个体，后者也会发出警报，有时会形成一种连锁反应。成群的无眼犬可能非常危险。不过，它们典型地笨拙，经常错误猜测猎物的确切位置。

entries.source:
Manticoils

Sigurd's danger level: 0%

Scientific name: Quadrupes-manta
Mantacoils are a passerine bird of the family corvidae. Their bodies are quite large compared to their early descendants, and their wingspan ranges from 55 to 64 inches. Their most defining characteristic is their set of four wings. Their back wings are mostly used to stabilize when at low speed, while their front two wings create the majority of lift. Their round bodies are a striking yellow but with black outlines or stripes along their primary (rear) feathers.

Manticoils mostly feed on small insects but can also feed on small rodents. They are highly intelligent and social. They pose little threat and have a generally passive temperament towards humans, although they are capable of transmitting Rabies, Rubenchloria, and Pitt Virus.
entries.target:
曼提柯鸟

西格德危险等级：0%

学名：Quadrupes-manta
曼提柯鸟是鸦科的一种雀形目鸟类。与早期后代相比，它们的身体相当大，翼展在 55 到 64 英寸之间。它们最显著的特征是四只翅膀。后翅主要在低速时用于稳定，而前两只翅膀提供大部分升力。它们圆滚滚的身体呈醒目的黄色，主羽（后羽）边缘或条纹为黑色。

曼提柯鸟主要以小昆虫为食，也会捕食小型啮齿动物。它们高度聪明且具有社会性。它们威胁很小，通常对人类表现被动，不过它们能够传播狂犬病、Rubenchloria 和 Pitt 病毒。

entries.source:
Spore lizard

Sigurd's danger level: i ,dont know probably 5% i just hate this pudgy legged little sh it

Scientific name: Lacerta-glomerorum
Colloquially named puffers or spore lizards, Lacerta-glomerorum (of the family Alligatoridae) is one of the largest and heaviest reptiles. Despite their large mouths, they are herbivores and do not have a strong bite. The bulbs on their tails are believed to secrete a chemical which attracts and accelerates the growth of the fungus species Lycoperdon perlatum, which it can then shake to release spores as a defense mechanism--an unique example of a mutualistic symbiotic relationship.

Spore lizards have a very timid temperment, tending to avoid all confrontation if possible. If their attempts at threat display are not effective, they may attempt to attack, so it's not recommended to corner or chase one. There are historical records that spore lizards were at least partially domesticated hundreds of years ago, however this effort was set aside by an initiative to harvest their tails for their medicinal properties.
entries.target:
孢子蜥蜴

西格德危险等级：我，不知道大概 5% 吧，我就是讨厌这个胖腿小混蛋

学名：Lacerta-glomerorum
Lacerta-glomerorum 俗称气囊蜥或孢子蜥蜴，属于短吻鳄科，是最大也最重的爬行动物之一。尽管嘴很大，它们是食草动物，咬合力并不强。其尾部的球状囊被认为会分泌一种化学物质，吸引并促进马勃菌 Lycoperdon perlatum 生长，随后它可以摇动尾部释放孢子作为防御机制，这是互利共生关系中一个独特例子。

孢子蜥蜴性情非常胆小，通常尽可能避免所有冲突。如果它们的威吓展示没有效果，可能会尝试攻击，因此不建议将其逼入角落或追逐。有历史记录显示，孢子蜥蜴在数百年前至少曾被部分驯化，但后来由于一项采集其尾部药用成分的计划，这一努力被搁置。

SNARE FLEA

Scientific name: Dolus-scolopendra. 
A very large arthropod of the class chilopoda. Its body produces a silk which it primarily uses to propel itself to places where it is concealed. Its exoskeleton is somewhat fragile, and they can die from long falls. The snare flea does not produce venom, nor does it have a strong bite. It makes up for this weakness with its ability to tighten itself around large prey to suffocate.

The snare flea thrives in dark, warm areas. It cannot survive low temperatures and generally avoids open air and sunlight take the rats outside or just beat the hell otu of them! i think their insides could make a good milkshake,,

一种非常巨大的唇足纲节肢动物。它的身体会产生丝，主要用来将自己推进到隐蔽位置。它的外骨骼相当脆弱，长距离坠落可能致死。抱脸虫不会产生毒液，也没有强力咬合。它用勒紧大型猎物使其窒息的能力弥补这些弱点。

entries.source:
Thumpers

Sigurd's danger level: 90%

Scientific name: Pistris-saevus
Halves, or Thumpers, are a highly aggressive, carnivorous species of the order Chondrichthyes. Their skeletons are cartilaginous, giving their bodies a stretchy and rubbery quality. They must eat their bottom legs in order to escape the shell of their hatched egg; their bottom legs are hardly functional to begin with. Their arms, or front legs, are very strong, and they occasionally use them to stomp prey. They can reach great speeds in a straight line.

They are relentless hunters, typically at the top of their food chain. Their main weaknesses are their intelligence and complete lack of hearing. If you come across a thumper, your best means of survival are leaving its line of sight, as it is slower around corners and can't easily track prey.

Due to the fast and volatile evolution of this species, some theorize that thumpers are one of the examples of an increased number of mutations causing higher levels of speciation in planets around the Thistle Nebula.
entries.target:
重锤兽

西格德危险等级：90%

学名：Pistris-saevus
半身兽，或称重锤兽，是软骨鱼纲中一种高度攻击性的食肉物种。它们的骨骼由软骨构成，使身体具有拉伸和橡胶般的质感。为了从孵化卵壳中逃脱，它们必须吃掉自己的下肢；这些下肢本来就几乎没有功能。它们的手臂，或者说前肢，非常强壮，偶尔会用来踩踏猎物。它们可以在直线上达到很高速度。

它们是执着的猎手，通常处于食物链顶端。它们的主要弱点是智力低下以及完全没有听觉。如果遇到重锤兽，最好的生存方式是离开它的视线，因为它在转角处会变慢，也不容易追踪猎物。

由于该物种快速且不稳定的进化，一些人推测重锤兽是蓟状星云周边行星上突变数量增加、导致物种形成水平提高的例子之一。

entries.source:
Tulip Snakes

Sigurd's danger level: 1%

Scientific name: Draco tulipa
Tulip snakes, or tulip lizards, are a type of flying lizard which are easily distinguished by their long arms and wings, which can reach twice the size of other members of their genus (Draco) and sport unusually bright colors and patterns. Their name comes from the flaps under their neck and behind their head, which resemble large flower petals. (Their other unusual characteristics include an extra pair of eyes and a deeply forked tail.)

Their behaviour is stubborn and fearless to a fault. Indeed, tulip snakes will rarely flee a predator. But ironically, many biologists theorize that their flowery appearance aids them more as a camoflauge than an intimidation display. It also takes an important part in their elaborate courtship rituals, which are more similar to that of birds. 

Uniquely, lifting heavy and large objects into the air has often been observed as a part of the tulip snake's mating display; they are capable of carrying rocks and plants at least twice their size in an effort to impress their potential mate. Protective, jealous, and in a frenzy, the males may end up in tug-of-war fights over objects they take a liking to, which can even include other living creatures.
entries.target:
郁金香蛇

西格德危险等级：1%

学名：Draco tulipa
郁金香蛇，或称郁金香蜥蜴，是一种飞行蜥蜴。它们很容易通过修长的前臂和翅膀辨认出来，其翅膀可达到同属 Draco 其他成员的两倍大小，并拥有异常鲜艳的颜色和花纹。它们的名字来自颈部下方和头后方的皮瓣，看起来像巨大的花瓣。（其他异常特征包括额外一对眼睛和深深分叉的尾巴。）

它们的行为固执且近乎鲁莽地无所畏惧。事实上，郁金香蛇很少会逃离捕食者。但讽刺的是，许多生物学家推测，它们花朵般的外观更多是作为伪装，而非威吓展示。这种外观也在它们复杂的求偶仪式中扮演重要角色，而这种仪式更类似鸟类。

独特的是，人们经常观察到将沉重的大型物体抬到空中是郁金香蛇求偶展示的一部分；它们能搬起至少两倍于自身体型的石块和植物，以吸引潜在配偶。雄性具有保护欲、嫉妒心，并会陷入狂热，可能围绕它们看中的物体展开拔河式争斗，这些物体甚至可能包括其他活物。

entries.source:
Baboon hawk

Sigurd's danger level: 75%

Scientific name: Papio-volturius
Baboon hawks are a primate of the family Cercopithecidae. They are hunchbacked but can stand up to 8 feet on average. Their heads are boney, with bird-like beaks and long horns, which they use like skewers to gore and feed on prey. Their horns are made of keratin instead of bone like the rest of their skulls, and they do not contain nerves or blood vessels. As a result baboon hawks can often break their horns from the force they apply, then fully regrow them within the same season. Baboon hawks partly owe their name to their large wings, which could never carry their large body mass and are used instead for intimidation and protection from the elements.

The largest baboon hawk troop ever observed was made up of 18 baboon hawks. They are loosely territorial, and much of their behavior is motivated by intimidation and display. They can become collectors, using any flashy or colorful object to mark their territory. As lone scouts, baboon hawks are generally timid and won't attack unless provoked. In greater numbers they can become a great danger; sticking close to others and making yourself seem dangerous are the best ways to prevent an attack. They prefer smaller mammals, but when desperate they are known to use their numbers to attack animals even twice their size, such as eyeless dogs. THEY TOOK M Y PICKLES
entries.target:
狒狒鹰

西格德危险等级：75%

学名：Papio-volturius
狒狒鹰是猴科的一种灵长动物。它们驼背，但平均能站到 8 英尺高。它们的头部多骨质，长着鸟喙般的嘴和长角，会像用叉子一样用角刺穿并捕食猎物。它们的角由角蛋白构成，不像头骨其余部分那样是骨头，也不含神经或血管。因此，狒狒鹰常会因自己施加的力量折断角，然后在同一季节内完全再生。狒狒鹰的名字部分来自它们巨大的翅膀；这些翅膀永远无法承载它们庞大的体重，反而用于威吓以及抵御自然环境。

有记录以来观察到的最大狒狒鹰群由 18 只狒狒鹰组成。它们具有松散的领地意识，大部分行为都由威吓和展示驱动。它们可能会变成收藏者，用任何闪亮或鲜艳的物体标记领地。作为独行侦察者时，狒狒鹰通常胆小，除非被激怒，否则不会攻击。数量增多时，它们会变得十分危险；靠近同伴并让自己显得危险，是避免遭受攻击的最佳方式。它们偏好较小型哺乳动物，但在绝望时，已知会凭借数量攻击体型甚至两倍于自己的动物，例如无眼犬。它们拿走了我的腌黄瓜

entries.source:
Bracken -- AKA flower man!

it was flower man, you can't say othrwise! i wanted to find his body .You were the cowards

Scientific name: Rapax-folium
There is debate on the genus to which the Bracken belongs. It is a bipedal vertebrate with skin the color and texture of a red beat. The name was coined for what appear to be leaves protruding from its upper spine. The purpose of these is believed to be for intimidation, however not much is known about the specifics of bracken behaviour due to its illusiveness and low population.

We know a little from accounts by wildlife experts who have encountered it. is a lone hunter with high intelligence. Its behavior can seem aloof; it exhibits high aggression even when unprovoked, yet it quickly backs off when confronted. However, brackens are known to up their hostility when cornered or simply watched for a long time. For this reason it's recommended to keep an eye on it but not to stare it down. No specimens have been collected dead or alive. It's theorized that upon death their bodies undergo a rapid decomposition process which is unique from other large animals.
entries.target:
花人 -- 又名 flower man！

就是花人，你不能说不是！我想找到他的尸体。你们才是懦夫

学名：Rapax-folium
关于花人所属的属仍有争议。它是一种双足脊椎动物，皮肤的颜色和质感类似红甜菜。这个名字来自其上脊柱处似乎突出的叶片。据信这些叶片用于威吓，但由于花人行踪隐秘且种群数量很低，人们对其行为细节知之甚少。

我们从曾遭遇过它的野生动物专家记录中了解了一些情况。它是具有高智力的独行猎手。它的行为可能显得冷淡；即使未受挑衅，也会表现出高度攻击性，但一旦被正面面对，又会迅速退开。然而，已知花人在被逼入角落或被长时间注视时会提高敌意。因此建议盯住它，但不要死死凝视。尚未收集到死亡或存活的标本。据推测，它们死亡后身体会经历一种快速分解过程，这一点有别于其他大型动物。

entries.source:
Bunker spiders

Sigurd's danger level: 30%

Scientific name: Mutinum-ficedula
Bunker spiders, of the genus Theraphosa, are the largest arachnid found in the Thistle Nebula and the second largest ever discovered. It's believed they evolved to prey on large mammals over the course of a measly several hundred years after the Boat made its trip around the Thistle Nebula. (Refer to: Speculation on Increased Speciation Around the Fading Nebulae)

Bunker spiders produce silk and lay it around their chosen nesting area, then wait for it to be tripped on. They can be seen waiting on walls, often over doorways where prey could enter unaware. If you find a bunker spider 'unprepared' it may freeze as a defensive reaction. In this case they are best left alone. If a bunker spider reacts aggressively, it is best not to fight with ordinary tools. They use their webs to make up for their rather slow movement, so take note of your surroundings. Their webs can be broken easily with any blunt tool.

Bunker spiders can pose a great danger to humans and urban explorers especially, without a great benefit to their ecosystems. A resulting kill-on-sight order was informally agreed upon between many states home to the Bunker spider, until it was approved by the ITDA as of 10/6/1965 and remains in effect.
entries.target:
地堡蜘蛛

西格德危险等级：30%

学名：Mutinum-ficedula
地堡蜘蛛属于 Theraphosa 属，是蓟状星云中发现的最大蛛形纲动物，也是有史以来发现的第二大蛛形纲动物。据信，在“船”绕蓟状星云航行后短短数百年间，它们进化为捕食大型哺乳动物。（参见：关于衰退星云周边物种形成增加的推测）

地堡蜘蛛会产丝，并将蛛丝布置在自己选定的筑巢区域周围，然后等待猎物触碰。它们常被看到在墙上等待，尤其是在门口上方，猎物可能毫无察觉地进入。如果你发现一只地堡蜘蛛“尚未准备好”，它可能会因防御反应而僵住。在这种情况下，最好不要招惹它。如果地堡蜘蛛表现出攻击性，最好不要用普通工具与其战斗。它们利用蛛网弥补自己相当缓慢的移动速度，因此请留意周围环境。任何钝器都能轻易破坏它们的蛛网。

地堡蜘蛛会对人类，尤其是城市探险者，构成极大危险，同时对其生态系统并无太大益处。因此，许多地堡蜘蛛栖息地所在的国家曾非正式同意“见到即处决”命令，直到 1965 年 10 月 6 日该命令获得 ITDA 批准，并一直生效至今。

entries.source:
Circuit Bees

Sigurd's danger level: 90%

Scientific name: Crabro-coruscus
The circuit bee, also known as red bee, is a eusocial flying insect of the genus Apis, a descendent of the honey bee. Their appearance is quite recognizable from their hairy, red bodies and two sets of wings. Like their ancestors, they are well-known for their intelligent social BEEhavior, large colony size, building wax nests which they use to store honey, and their important role in pollination. Unlike the honey bee, which often chose high places such as trees to construct its hive, red bees create their hives on the ground.

Red bees are highly defensive. They will leave the nest to attack any creature that comes within several meters, leaving BEEhind only the queen and drone bees. This bold BEEhavior is enabled by their most defining aBEElity, which is their electrostatic charge. Red bees produce friction with the air. They also produce friction by rubbing their two pairs of wings against each other and by rubbing against one another while in the hive. What allows them to create such a surplus of electric field compared to the honey bee is still under research, as they generate a stronger electric field when panicked or angered. This ability is especially useful for them around water.



It's BEEst to keep your distance. If a red bee hive is stolen, red bee swarms will enter an onslaught in which they attack any living creature. This destructive BEEhavior will last until they have located the hive or completely exhausted themselves, which can take hours to days. They have BEEn known to leave BEEhind fields of bodies of small rodents, insects and even some larger mammals, and in rare cases they can start fires. Their strong BEEnefits and drawbacks to their ecosystems are highly debated. BEEbated !! - the indomitable Sigurd
entries.target:
电路蜂

西格德危险等级：90%

学名：Crabro-coruscus
电路蜂，又称红蜂，是蜜蜂属中一种真社会性飞行昆虫，是蜜蜂的后代。它们毛茸茸的红色身体和两对翅膀非常容易辨认。和祖先一样，它们以聪明的社会性蜂为、庞大的群体规模、建造用于储蜜的蜡巢以及在授粉中的重要作用而闻名。不同于常选择树木等高处筑巢的蜜蜂，红蜂会在地面筑巢。

红蜂防御性极强。任何生物只要靠近巢穴数米内，它们都会离巢攻击，只把蜂后和雄蜂留在蜂后面。这种大胆蜂为得益于它们最具代表性的能蜂，也就是静电荷。红蜂会与空气摩擦产生电荷。它们也会通过两对翅膀互相摩擦，以及在巢内彼此摩擦来产生电荷。与蜜蜂相比，它们为什么能形成如此过量的电场仍在研究中；当它们恐慌或愤怒时，会产生更强电场。这种能力在水域附近尤其有用。



最好和它们保持距离。如果红蜂巢被偷，红蜂群会进入一场猛攻，攻击任何活物。这种破坏性蜂为会持续到它们找到蜂巢，或彻底耗尽体力为止，可能持续数小时到数天。已知它们会留下成片小型啮齿动物、昆虫，甚至一些较大哺乳动物的尸体，少数情况下还能引发火灾。它们对生态系统的巨大蜂益和缺点仍存在高度争议。蜂议！！ - 不屈的 Sigurd

entries.source:
Kidnapper fox

Sigurd's danger level: 80% GOOD JOB he gets an A+ in creep schcool

Scientific name: Vulpes raptor
A large mammal of the family Canidae, the Kidnapper fox has many unique biological traits. Two of these are its horizontal jaw and unique skull shape giving it a great biting strength, and its long, frog-like tongue which can reach even twice the length of its spindly body. They are solitary hunters and mate for life.

Kidnapper foxes have a very long, bushy neck and tail with a more reddish coloration, which is thought to blend in well with the numerous Vain Shroud, where it can almost always be found hiding and waiting for prey. The Kidnapper fox and Vain shrouds together form one of the most infamously known and unique mutualistic relationships between two invasive species. Together they continue to spread across planetary systems, stowing away on large spacecraft. Excluding a select few nature preserves, the ITDA has issued a kill-on-sight order for the Kidnapper fox and Vain shrouds due to their impact on ecosystems. 

The Kidnapper fox earns its name from its aggressive yet timid and territorial behaviour, preferring to finish off prey out of sight and usually in the comfort of the Vain shrouds. It usually chooses to lie low and wait for things to come its way. But if it grows restless, it can use its long, sticky tongue to snatch its prey from great distances and drag them as far as it needs. If there are Vain shrouds in your area, carry a weapon and do not stray far from others; its tongue is quite soft and fragile and can be struck easily, forcing it to stop an assault. Today there are relatively few human deaths attributed to Kidnapper foxes per year, and most victims are unsupervised children or adults hiking alone.
entries.target:
绑架狐

西格德危险等级：80% 干得漂亮，他在恐怖学校能拿 A+

学名：Vulpes raptor
绑架狐是一种犬科大型哺乳动物，拥有许多独特的生物特征。其中两个是横向开合的颌部和独特的头骨形状，使其咬合力极强；以及细长、蛙类般的舌头，长度甚至能达到其瘦长身体的两倍。它们是独居猎手，并且终生配偶制。

绑架狐有非常长而蓬松的颈部和尾巴，颜色更偏红，被认为能很好地融入大量虚荣红蕨之中。它几乎总是藏在这些植物里，等待猎物。绑架狐和虚荣红蕨共同构成了两种入侵物种之间最臭名昭著且独特的互利关系之一。它们一起继续跨行星系统扩散，偷渡在大型航天器上。除少数自然保护区外，ITDA 已因绑架狐和虚荣红蕨对生态系统的影响，对二者发布见到即处决命令。

绑架狐因其攻击性、胆怯且具有领地意识的行为而得名，它偏好在视线外解决猎物，通常是在虚荣红蕨的舒适掩护中。它通常选择潜伏，等待猎物自己靠近。但如果它变得焦躁，就能用又长又黏的舌头从很远处抓住猎物，并把猎物拖到所需位置。如果你所在区域有虚荣红蕨，请携带武器，不要离他人太远；它的舌头相当柔软脆弱，很容易被击中，迫使它停止攻击。如今每年归因于绑架狐的人类死亡相对较少，大多数受害者是无人看护的儿童或独自徒步的成年人。

entries.source:
Cadaver Growths / Cadaver Blooms

Sigurd's danger level: 100% they made me drink weed killer and tthrow up and then drink weed killer again and then throw up again. im never going back to admance THEY WERE JUST PRETTY FLOWERS

Scientific name: Moderantum flos
Cadaver Growths, also known as Cadavers, are a dangerous, aggressively-growing fern which thrives in dark, wet and enclosed spaces such as caves. They are recognizable as clusters of tall ferns, combined with large, sickly-yellow petals of a flowering plant. Do not enter a room where Cadaver Growths are present. Even if you have received the vaccine, intense exposure to Cadaver spores can overwhelm the immune system's antibodies.

These yellow petals are now a vestigial structure previously belonging to the species Pilea microphylla or the Artillery Plant, which has been co-opted by the Cadaver Growth for dispersal of its spores. There is some debate among plant biologists whether this is still two species, but the general consensus is that the fern assimilated this flowering species and now shares much of its DNA. The specifics of this process are still under confidential research. 

Because of this successful hijacking and its already-intense ability to self-replicate, the fern is able to spread its spores at incredible efficiency and is well-equipped to attempt to perform a similar process in mammals, including humans. The resulting infected creatures are called Cadaver Blooms.

At the point in which spores have entered the body and are beginning to germinate within the lungs, prevention is still possible, and anyone in contact should immediately seek medical attention. The first, most common sign is small, yellow petals sticking out from the neck or back. In desperate circumstances, various herbicides have been found to kill the infection in early stages. If the victim is emitting spores visibly from the mouth, full treatment is almost impossible, and anyone in proximity should cover their faces and keep a distance of at least 10 feet; this sign of critical infection shows in about 80% of cases. 

Once the infection has become critical, if the host loses consciousness for any reason, the plant will take immediate, full control, accelerating growth using the body's nutrients. If the host does not lose consciousness, the plant will eventually shut down the brain by crowding it out with its own growths. It's not fully understood why the growth "waits" for the brain to enter a state of unconsciousness, but this has lent to the name Cadaver Bloom, due to the manner in which it activates at death. Luckily, no outbreak of Cadaver Blooms has been recorded to jump from the species of origin thus far.

Any sighting of Cadaver Growths must be reported to the ITDA.
entries.target:
尸体生长体 / 尸体花

西格德危险等级：100% 他们让我喝除草剂然后吐出来，再喝除草剂然后又吐出来。我再也不回 adamance 了，它们只是漂亮的花

学名：Moderantum flos
尸体生长体，也称尸体，是一种危险且侵略性生长的蕨类植物，繁盛于黑暗、潮湿、封闭的空间，例如洞穴。它们可以通过成簇高大蕨类与大片病态黄色花瓣的开花植物组合辨认出来。不要进入存在尸体生长体的房间。即使你已接种疫苗，强烈暴露于尸体孢子中也可能压垮免疫系统的抗体。

这些黄色花瓣如今是一种退化结构，原本属于 Pilea microphylla，也就是炮仗草，该物种已被尸体生长体劫持，用于传播自身孢子。植物生物学家之间仍有争议，认为这是否仍是两个物种，但普遍共识是，这种蕨类同化了该开花物种，现在与其共享大量 DNA。该过程的具体细节仍处于保密研究中。

由于这种成功劫持以及本身强烈的自我复制能力，这种蕨类能以惊人的效率传播孢子，并且非常适合尝试在哺乳动物，包括人类身上执行类似过程。由此产生的感染生物被称为尸体花。

当孢子进入身体并开始在肺部萌发时，预防仍然可能，任何接触者都应立即寻求医疗帮助。最初且最常见的征兆，是颈部或背部伸出小型黄色花瓣。在绝望情况下，多种除草剂已被发现可在早期阶段杀死感染。如果受害者口中可见地喷出孢子，完整治疗几乎不可能，附近所有人都应遮住面部，并保持至少 10 英尺距离；这种关键感染征兆出现在约 80% 的病例中。

一旦感染进入关键阶段，如果宿主因任何原因失去意识，植物将立即完全接管，利用身体营养加速生长。如果宿主没有失去意识，植物最终会以自身生长物挤占大脑，使其停止运作。目前尚不完全理解这种生长物为何会“等待”大脑进入无意识状态，但这也促成了尸体花这个名称，因为它会以死亡时激活的方式绽放。幸运的是，至今没有记录显示尸体花疫情从原宿主物种跳跃传播。

任何尸体生长体目击事件都必须上报 ITDA。

entries.source:
Feiopar

Scientific name: Felis arborum

The Feiopar is a large mammal of the family Saeptivus. They typically stand at 5 feet tall. Of all species in this family, they most resemble their ancestor felines, though they differentiate themselves with their bony backs and long necks. Their tails are also twice the length of other species, commonly thought to support their enhanced climbing abilities, which they put to use when threatened. Feiopars are a recent discovery exclusive to the Green Witches--Vow, March, and Adamance.

Their personalities are very timid yet curious. Although they may appear often to travelers and campers--seated low in the dense brush--they are rarely dangerous, and even their attacks are rarely fatal. There are no recorded deaths attributed to Feiopars. Hikers and explorers have linked the Feiopar to multiple urban legends of the past and rumor the Feiopar to be behind many disappearances, specifically on Vow. However, these rumors are unfounded. The Feiopar is a vital regulator of these ecosystems and is only curious about the new visitors of its territory. So if you are planning on a trip to the remote and scenic Vow, you might want to learn some do's and don'ts about your small, saeptival friend!

First of all: never hike alone. It's always good to have a buddy to watch your back, and Feiopars are intimidated by groups. And of course, never leave food out where it will attract prey and predators alike. Always keep your food in sealed containers. Side note: recent studies have indicated Feiopars are somewhat agitated by the color orange and high-visibility markings, however these have not been well-established.

If you do encounter a Feiopar, do not panic. Pick up small children and stay calm. Speak in a low voice, make yourself look as big as possible, and maintain direct eye contact. Unlike most predators, maintaining direct eye contact with the Feiopar is an essential deterrence. If you cannot locate the Feiopar, back away slowly and scan the forest for its bright, yellow eyes. When approaching, the Feiopar will typically give itself away with a brief, low purring. If this sound has already gone silent, the Feiopar may be within a dangerous distance. At very close proximity, eye contact with the Feiopar can actually have an adverse effect and persuade the Feiopar to pounce. This is why it's always recommended to keep non-lethal self defense such as bear spray on hand. If an encounter with the Feiopar has become violent, onlookers should stay clear and immediately seek medical aid for those involved, as attacks are often short-lived.
entries.target:
Feiopar

学名：Felis arborum

Feiopar 是 Saeptivus 科的一种大型哺乳动物。它们通常高 5 英尺。在该科所有物种中，它们最像祖先猫科动物，但以骨质背部和长颈与之区分。它们的尾巴也有其他物种的两倍长，通常被认为用于支持增强的攀爬能力，并在受威胁时加以利用。Feiopar 是一种近来才发现的物种，仅见于“绿色女巫”：Vow、March 和 Adamance。

它们的性格非常胆怯却好奇。尽管旅行者和露营者可能经常看见它们低伏在浓密灌木中，但它们很少危险，即使攻击也很少致命。没有任何记录显示死亡事件归因于 Feiopar。徒步者和探险者将 Feiopar 与过去多个都市传说联系起来，并传言 Feiopar 是许多失踪案背后的原因，尤其是在 Vow 上。然而，这些传言并无根据。Feiopar 是这些生态系统的重要调节者，只是对进入其领地的新访客感到好奇。因此，如果你计划前往偏远而风景优美的 Vow，也许应该了解一些关于这位小型 saeptival 朋友的注意事项！

首先：永远不要独自徒步。有人替你留意背后总是好事，而且 Feiopar 会被群体吓退。当然，永远不要把食物放在外面，吸引猎物和捕食者。务必将食物保存在密封容器中。旁注：近期研究表明，Feiopar 似乎会被橙色和高可视标记稍微激怒，但这些结论尚未充分确立。

如果你确实遇到 Feiopar，不要惊慌。抱起小孩并保持冷静。低声说话，让自己看起来尽可能大，并保持直接眼神接触。不同于大多数捕食者，与 Feiopar 保持直接眼神接触是一种关键威慑。如果你无法确定 Feiopar 的位置，请缓慢后退，并在森林中寻找它明亮的黄色眼睛。接近时，Feiopar 通常会以短促低沉的呼噜声暴露自身。如果这种声音已经停止，Feiopar 可能已处于危险距离内。在极近距离下，与 Feiopar 进行眼神接触反而可能产生相反效果，促使它扑击。因此，始终建议随身携带熊喷雾等非致命自卫工具。如果与 Feiopar 的遭遇变为暴力事件，旁观者应远离，并立即为相关人员寻求医疗援助，因为攻击通常持续时间很短。

entries.source:
Maneater

Sigurd's danger level: 1000%

yOU WERE LIKE A SON

Scientific name: Periplaneta clamorus

The Maneater has been linked to a genus of cockroaches known as Periplaneta. For many, the creature still cannot be separated from its mythos, and as such, a full description must include a brief historical summary. 

Before its discovery, it was first nicknamed by workers of the Caswella Mine--one of many copper mines along the asteroid belt of Lepus, in 1951. The Caswella Mine had begun to languish since the end of wartime and would soon close after operating for twelve years; working conditions had worsened long before the rumor of the Maneater had begun to spread, and as a result, it was not taken seriously by the higher ups or media at the time. On June 23 of that year, however, an eviscerated corpse was rescued from the lowest level of Caswella Mine. 

It was later identified as Brett Goldent, a senior mining operator. Autopsy recorded the death an accident resulting from faulty equipment. However the story became a sensation, and the Maneater rose up as a famous myth until six years later, when it would be first recorded in the DEEP57 cave expedition. The mission was headed by the son of Brett Goldent, Simon Goldent, to put to rest the mystery of his father's death. Simon, who had seeked to disprove the myth, described his discovery as "traumatic." 

This was a revelation to the scientific world. At the time, a creature of this size confined to underground caves was thought impossible, and it is now known as the largest troglobite (cave-dwelling species). 

Periplaneta clamorus is thought to live off of mainly plant debri and animal carcasses, both of which can be carried into the caves by running water. It seems to have never developed the instinctive fear of larger predators while living underground. Thus, as a larva it may be fascinated or indifferent to human presence. However, as larvae, they can react fearfully to loud noises or to the sight of blood and injury. Some researchers have observed the larvae to show signs of loneliness.

Being unhappy or afraid seems to exacerbate Periplaneta clamorus' symptoms of growth. Its metamorphosis is remarkably sudden and is a dangerous phenomenon to observe. The most telling sign that a larva is approaching metamorphosis is an excretion of an acidic, foamy liquid at its lower jaw; this substance is believed to be a catalyst and exits its body in large quantities once metamorphosis begins.

An adult can move incredibly fast and is capable of creating loud noise by clicking its mandibles at high frequency. In an encounter with Periplaneta clamorus, recommended procedure is to back away slowly and keep your distance, as it seems to wait for you to approach and avoids line of sight. Stopping and exhaling suddenly while arching its legs backwards is a sign that an adult is entering chase. God help[ you
entries.target:
食人者

西格德危险等级：1000%

你曾经像个儿子

学名：Periplaneta clamorus

食人者与名为 Periplaneta 的蟑螂属有关。对许多人来说，这种生物仍无法与其神话传说分开，因此完整描述必须包含一段简短历史概述。

在被发现之前，1951 年，Lepus 小行星带沿线众多铜矿之一的 Caswella 矿井工人最先给它起了绰号。Caswella 矿井自战争结束后便开始衰败，并将在运营十二年后很快关闭；早在食人者传言开始传播之前，工作条件就已恶化，因此当时高层和媒体并未认真对待这些传闻。然而，同年 6 月 23 日，一具被剖开的尸体从 Caswella 矿井最底层被救出。

后来确认死者为高级采矿操作员 Brett Goldent。尸检记录将死亡归为设备故障导致的事故。然而，这个故事引发轰动，食人者作为著名传说兴起，直到六年后，它在 DEEP57 洞穴探险中首次被正式记录。该任务由 Brett Goldent 的儿子 Simon Goldent 领导，旨在终结其父死亡之谜。Simon 曾试图证伪这一传说，却将自己的发现描述为“创伤性的”。

这对科学界而言是一次启示。当时，人们认为如此体型的生物不可能被限制在地下洞穴中，而如今它被称为最大的洞穴生物。

Periplaneta clamorus 被认为主要依靠植物碎屑和动物尸体为生，两者都可被流水带入洞穴。它似乎从未在地下生活中发展出对大型捕食者的本能恐惧。因此，幼虫可能会对人类存在感到着迷或漠然。然而，作为幼虫时，它们会对巨响或血液、伤口景象产生恐惧反应。一些研究者观察到幼虫表现出孤独迹象。

不快乐或恐惧似乎会加剧 Periplaneta clamorus 的生长症状。其变态过程极其突然，是一种危险的观察现象。幼虫接近变态最明显的征兆，是下颌处分泌酸性泡沫状液体；这种物质被认为是催化剂，并会在变态开始后大量排出体外。

成体移动速度极快，并能够通过高频咬合下颚制造巨大噪声。遭遇 Periplaneta clamorus 时，建议步骤是缓慢后退并保持距离，因为它似乎会等待你靠近并避开视线。突然停下、呼气，并将腿向后弓起，是成体进入追逐状态的信号。上帝保佑[你

entries.source:
OLD BIRDS

 The Old Bird is an autonomous, offensive weapon of war with a humanoid design. Measured at 19 feet tall and 11 feet wide, its two most defining visual features are the spotlight positioned on its head, capable of emitting 100,000 lumens, and the long-range acoustic device on its chest, sometimes referred to as a sound cannon. There are additional speakers on its shoulders used to broadcast sound a long distance. The Old Bird's left arm is a claw, and its right arm is a nozzle capable of both launching rocket-propelled grenades and torching at close-range with a very hot flame. Old Birds are one of the first extra-orbital weapons to be used and mass-produced.\r
 The subject of who developed the Old Birds has been an intense debate since their first recorded appearance on December 18 of 2143, when a large number of Old Birds invaded the

The design of the Old Bird, down to the tempering of metals, appears intended to conceal its origin. It has been called the "walking ransom letter." \r
 Old Birds' legs function as rockets, which allows them to travel long distances and find targets efficiently. But the most likely reason this feature exists is to help them enter and land from orbit. The material and fuel Old Birds rely on is similar to those of passenger space craft from around 2130.\r
Old Birds land and stay at their target planet forever. Although they often have more than enough fuel for just one trip, no behavior has been found in their programming which suggests they could choose to "migrate". Still, there are unverified accounts of Old Birds travelling autonomously to a new planet after hundreds of years of dormancy. \r
The Old Bird was historically referred to as the "Al", based off the code name it was given by the English military, the "A16-L31". In 2384, however, the song "Old Birds" released as a cult hit by the post-punk project Under Remora; just three years later, in his most famous work of the same name, street artist Land Eyrie depicted the automotons flying and landing between planets in an arrangement similar to a flock of geese--this is generally considered one of the most iconic and influential images of modern culture and solidified the modern nickname.\r
 In 2356, 5-Embrion was classified as Condemned for the purposes of travel or settlement by the Boat; Old Birds were described by witness accounts as "lining the horizon". Though to this day they appear inoperative, there is a reasonable risk that many are still in sleep mode, so the planet will likely keep its status. The small moon was likely used as a testing ground for the Old Bird's flying and landing capabilities.
entries.target:
老鸟

老鸟是一种自主进攻型战争武器，采用类人设计。其高度为 19 英尺，宽度为 11 英尺，最显著的两个视觉特征是头部的探照灯，可发出 100,000 流明光照，以及胸部的远程声学装置，有时被称为声炮。肩部还装有额外扬声器，用于远距离广播声音。老鸟的左臂是爪，右臂是喷嘴，既能发射火箭推进榴弹，也能在近距离喷射高温火焰。老鸟是最早被使用并大规模生产的外轨道武器之一。
关于老鸟由谁开发，自 2143 年 12 月 18 日首次记录出现以来一直争论激烈，当时大量老鸟入侵了

老鸟的设计，甚至包括金属回火方式，似乎都是为了隐藏其来源。它被称为“行走的勒索信”。
老鸟的腿具备火箭功能，使其能够长距离移动并高效寻找目标。但该功能存在的最可能原因，是帮助它们从轨道进入并着陆。老鸟依赖的材料和燃料与 2130 年左右的客运航天器相似。
老鸟会降落并永远停留在目标行星上。虽然它们通常有足够燃料进行不止一次航行，但没有发现其程序中存在会选择“迁徙”的行为。尽管如此，仍有未经证实的记录称，老鸟在数百年休眠后会自主前往新的行星。
老鸟历史上曾被称为“Al”，该名称源于英国军方给它的代号“A16-L31”。然而在 2384 年，后朋克项目 Under Remora 发布的歌曲《Old Birds》成为邪典热门；仅三年后，街头艺术家 Land Eyrie 在其同名最著名作品中描绘了这些自动机像一群鹅一样在行星间飞行和着陆，这通常被认为是现代文化中最具标志性和影响力的图像之一，也巩固了现代绰号。
2356 年，5-Embrion 被“船”列为不适合旅行或定居的废弃禁区；目击者描述老鸟“排列在地平线上”。尽管至今它们似乎处于失效状态，但仍有合理风险表明许多个体仍处于睡眠模式，因此该行星很可能会继续保持这一状态。这颗小卫星很可能曾被用作测试老鸟飞行和着陆能力的场地。

Giant Sapsucker

Sigurd's danger level: 10% NEVERMIND 80%

Scientific name: Sphyrapicus cursus 

The Giant Sapsucker is a woodpecker belonging to the genus Sphyrapicus, also known as sapsuckers. As the name suggests, they are by far the largest of the species. Males on average are 5 feet tall, while females vary anywhere from seven to nine feet. They are flightless birds, although they have a vestigial wing structure which is usually hidden by their plumage. Their bodies are brown or black, with white and red face markings. They are sharp-sighted, with nearly a 360 degree range of vision. A fully grown adult's body is one of the most athletically capable of all species in the forest planets it typically inhabits.

Their diet consists of insects and tree sap, with the inclusion of small rodents. Giant sapsuckers mate for life, and the adult male typically hunts and forages while the female protects the ground nest. They are fiercely protective mothers, partly due to their opportunistic nature, and if anything crosses their boundaries, they will attempt to feed it to their young in pieces. They are dangerous to humans as well, and it's recommended to stay out of their territory. 

They have been destructive to some forests and ecosystems; although they do not multiply as quickly as other species, they have been known as nomads, travelling very far very quickly, which makes their populations difficult to track. 

Richard is also a giant sapsucker
entries.target:
巨型吸汁啄木鸟

西格德危险等级：10% 不对 80%

学名：Sphyrapicus cursus

巨型吸汁啄木鸟是一种属于 Sphyrapicus 属的啄木鸟，也被称为吸汁啄木鸟。顾名思义，它们是该类物种中迄今为止最大的一种。雄性平均高 5 英尺，而雌性高度在 7 到 9 英尺之间不等。它们是不会飞的鸟类，尽管仍保留退化的翼结构，通常隐藏在羽毛下。它们的身体呈棕色或黑色，面部有白色和红色斑纹。它们目光锐利，视野范围接近 360 度。成年个体的身体是其通常栖息的森林行星中运动能力最强的物种之一。

它们以昆虫和树液为食，也会摄入小型啮齿动物。巨型吸汁啄木鸟终生配偶制，成年雄性通常负责狩猎和觅食，而雌性保护地面巢穴。它们是极具保护欲的母亲，部分源于机会主义天性；如果任何东西越过其边界，它们会试图将其撕成碎块喂给幼崽。它们对人类同样危险，建议远离其领地。

它们对一些森林和生态系统造成了破坏；尽管繁殖速度不如其他物种快，但它们以游牧习性闻名，能非常快速地迁徙很远距离，这使得种群难以追踪。

Richard 也是一只巨型吸汁啄木鸟

Vain Shrouds

Sigurd's danger level:  THIS IS NT A CREATURE.

Scientific name: Phlebodium ruber
Vain Shrouds, also commonly referred to as Vains, are a type of rhizomatous fern of the family Polypodiaceae. In ideal conditions, Vain Shrouds' fronds can reach a height of 8 feet, though they average 6'11'', and are 30-50 centimeters wide. Their roots are unusually large, and they are somewhat SOMEWHATTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTprickly. 

With their aggressive reproduction and remarkable size, they take up a lot of space, making visibility difficult, and are difficult to remove. They crowd out healthy plants and offer little nutritional value. They are also highly flammable. A strongly correlated mutualistic relationship has been observed between Vain Shrouds and the Kidnapper Fox, and for this reason Vains are a danger when they are allowed to expand to great numbers. Due to this and their other harmful effects on many ecosystems and agriculture, Vains have been classified as a noxious weed by the ITDA since 1963.

Their planet of origin is unknown, and they were first recorded by plant researcher Alex Kurt in CoRoT-7b in 1963 before being found on the other side of its same interplanetary system just weeks later. A common myth is that the Vains were first spread across planets due to their mild hallucinogenic properties when ingested, but their history is shrouded in mystery.

虚荣红蕨

西格德危险等级：这不是生物。

学名：Phlebodium ruber
虚荣红蕨，也常被称为 Vains，是水龙骨科的一种根茎蕨类。在理想条件下，虚荣红蕨的叶状体可达 8 英尺高，平均也有 6 英尺 11 英寸，宽度为 30 到 50 厘米。它们的根异常巨大，而且有点有点点点点点点点点点点点扎人。

凭借侵略性繁殖能力和惊人体型，它们会占据大量空间，使能见度变差，并且难以移除。它们挤占健康植物，几乎没有营养价值。它们也高度易燃。虚荣红蕨与绑架狐之间已观察到强相关的互利关系，因此当 Vains 扩张到大量数量时会变得危险。由于这一点以及它们对许多生态系统和农业造成的其他危害，Vains 自 1963 年起被 ITDA 归类为有害杂草。

它们的起源星球未知，最早由植物研究员 Alex Kurt 于 1963 年在 CoRoT-7b 记录，随后几周就在同一行星际系统的另一侧被发现。一个常见传说认为，Vains 最早因被摄入后具有轻微致幻特性而在各行星间传播，但其历史笼罩在谜团之中。

COIL-HEADS

Sigurd's danger level: 80%

Scientific name: Vir colligerus
Vir colligerus, or colloquially named Coil-heads, are a recent discovery and have not been studied extensively due to their extreme unpredictability and dangerous properties. They have been known to combust into flames when being dissected or even deactivated, and they carry dangerously high levels of radioactive particles. Due to this and other reasons, it has been highly speculated they were created as biological weapons of war, although this has not been proven.

Coil-heads' visual appearance is that of a bloody mannequin with its head connected by a spring. Their defining behavioral characteristic is to stop when being looked at. However, this does not appear to be a hard-and-fast rule. When they encounter a loud or bright light they sometimes appear to enter a long reset mode. 
Just stare at htem or use a stun grenade! - Sigurd
entries.target:
弹簧头

西格德危险等级：80%

学名：Vir colligerus
Vir colligerus，俗称弹簧头，是近期才发现的生物，由于其极端不可预测性和危险特性，尚未得到广泛研究。已知它们在被解剖甚至停用时会燃烧起火，并携带危险的高水平放射性粒子。由于这些以及其他原因，人们高度怀疑它们是作为战争生物武器被创造出来的，尽管这尚未得到证实。

弹簧头的外观像一个血淋淋的人体模型，头部由弹簧连接。它们最具代表性的行为特征，是被注视时会停止。然而，这似乎并不是一条绝对规则。当它们遇到巨大声响或强光时，有时似乎会进入较长的重置模式。
盯着它们看，或者用震撼弹！ - Sigurd

entries.source:
FOREST KEEPER

Sigurd's danger level: 50%

Scientific name: Satyrid-proceritas
Believed to share a common ancestor with rapax-folium, these behemoths are called Forest Keepers for the biomes they often inhabit. Their bodies bare markings on their front and back which mimic eyes--this trait is more helpful in their youth, as they are not agile. Their skin is a unique, dense material which hardens further throughout their lives; the large spikes and bumps across their bodies form as a result of aging.

It's been said Forest Keepers exhibit a curious behavior similar to that of a human child the age of 5 or 6. They will eat anything they find fascinating. Forest Keepers don't actually need to put anything into their mouths, and it's theorized their main source of energy is a process similar to photosynthesis. Still, this makes them relatively dangerous to observe. They can see across long distances, so staying low and making use of cover is a must. They cannot enter small spaces and are not generally destructive, so stay close to shelter or overhangs.
entries.target:
森林巨人

西格德危险等级：50%

学名：Satyrid-proceritas
这些庞然大物被认为与 rapax-folium 拥有共同祖先，并因其常栖息的生物群系而被称为森林巨人。它们身体前后有模仿眼睛的斑纹；这一特征在幼年时更有帮助，因为那时它们并不敏捷。它们的皮肤是一种独特而致密的材料，会在一生中继续硬化；遍布全身的大型尖刺和凸起则由衰老形成。

据说森林巨人会表现出类似 5、6 岁人类儿童的好奇行为。它们会吃掉任何让自己感兴趣的东西。森林巨人实际上并不需要把任何东西放进嘴里，理论认为它们主要的能量来源是类似光合作用的过程。尽管如此，这仍使它们成为相当危险的观察对象。它们能看见很远的距离，所以保持低姿态并利用掩护是必须的。它们无法进入狭小空间，通常也不具破坏性，因此请靠近遮蔽物或悬垂结构。

entries.source:
Backwater Gunkfish

Sigurd's danger level: 0% but these little goopy goblins wont leave me ALONE

Scientific name: Infectum linteum

The Backwater Gunkfish is a type of Gunkfish of the family Mobulidae. Unlike the other types of Gunkfish, the Backwater Gunkfish spends most of its time on land. It can inhabit any damp, warm space where a water source is present. Its body is fat, flat, squishy and gelatinous, the width of a doormat. Its tail, slightly harder than the rest of its soft body, can range from 2-3 feet in length. When stressed, its tail is often curled up over its head to position for firing mucus or to shake and rattle as a deterrent.

The Backwater Gunkfish feeds off organic material on surfaces, and for this reason, it is found most often around polluted water, swamps, or dirty sewers. When frightened, the Gunkfish can use an opening in its tail to release a burst of a thick, green mucus mixed with saliva to defend itself; the Backwater Gunkfish keeps an especially large "reserve" for emergency situations and can squirt multiple times. When needed, it uses part of this extra reserve to excrete from its body, increasing its mobility on land.

The Backwater Gunkfish is harmless and is considered a natural cleaner of its preferred environments. Its mucus is a disinfectant; though it is not safe or healthy to consume, it is unlikely to cause major harm if it enters the body through the eyes or nose. Its smell is often described as a mix of vinegar and lard, and it is slightly warm when freshly excreted.

Though not directly harmful, the Backwater Gunkfish is often considered a notorious pest to water treatment facilities and petroleum refineries. The gunkfish itself is evasive, capable of fitting itself into very small holes and pipes, and its mucus is arduous to remove once it has hardened, forming a substance comparable to ice. For all these reasons, the Backwater Gunkfish has lead to many accidents and should be taken seriously. Today, pest removal services are well-accustomed to safe Gunkfish removal and relocation operations, and there are many affordable options if you believe there is one in the area. The best way to prevent a Gunkfish infestation is to keep your environment clean and dry and to close off any access to standing water.
entries.target:
死水臭鱼

西格德危险等级：0%，但这些黏糊糊的小东西不会放过我

学名：Infectum linteum

死水臭鱼是蝠鲼科臭鱼的一种。不同于其他臭鱼，死水臭鱼大部分时间都待在陆地上。只要有水源存在，它可以栖息在任何潮湿温暖的空间中。它的身体肥胖、扁平、柔软且呈胶质，宽度约等于一块门垫。它的尾巴比身体其他柔软部位稍硬，长度可达 2 到 3 英尺。当感到压力时，它的尾巴常会卷到头顶上方，准备发射黏液，或通过摇晃和响动作为威慑。

死水臭鱼以表面上的有机物为食，因此最常见于受污染水域、沼泽或肮脏下水道附近。受惊时，臭鱼可以通过尾部开口释放混合唾液的浓稠绿色黏液来保护自己；死水臭鱼会为紧急情况保留特别大量的“储备”，并能多次喷射。必要时，它会用这部分额外储备从身体排出黏液，以提高在陆地上的机动性。

死水臭鱼无害，并被认为是其偏好环境中的天然清洁者。它的黏液是一种消毒剂；虽然摄入并不安全也不健康，但如果通过眼睛或鼻子进入体内，不太可能造成重大伤害。其气味常被描述为醋和猪油的混合，新鲜排出时略微温热。

尽管不直接有害，死水臭鱼常被视为水处理设施和炼油厂中臭名昭著的害虫。臭鱼本身善于躲避，能够钻进非常小的孔洞和管道，而它的黏液一旦硬化就很难清除，会形成类似冰的物质。由于所有这些原因，死水臭鱼已导致许多事故，必须认真对待。如今，害虫清除服务已非常熟悉安全移除和迁移臭鱼的操作，如果你认为附近有一只，也有许多价格合理的选择。预防臭鱼侵扰的最佳方式，是保持环境清洁干燥，并封闭任何通往积水的入口。

entries.source:
HOARDING BUG

Sigurd's danger level: 17%

Scientific name: Linepithema-crassus
Hoarding bugs (of the order Hymenoptera) are large, social insects. While often found living alone, they have been found to share their nests with members of their own species. They measure a height of 3 feet on average, with bulbous bodies. The thinness of their fluid and blood and the material of their carapaces contributes to their low weight, making them capable of flight with their membranous wings. It also makes their bodies somewhat transparent.

Hoarding bugs were given their name due to their territorial nature. Once they have chosen a place as their nest, they will seek to adorn it with any object they can find and will protect these objects as a part of the nest. Hoarding bugs are not so dangerous alone as they are in large hives. However, if left alone, hoarding bugs are surprisingly neutral and pose little danger. wWe love the stupid cuddle bugs.!! - tjhis has been a note from the indomitable Sigurd
entries.target:
囤积虫

西格德危险等级：17%

学名：Linepithema-crassus
囤积虫（膜翅目）是大型社会性昆虫。虽然常被发现独居，但它们也会与同种成员共享巢穴。它们平均身高 3 英尺，身体呈球状。其体液和血液稀薄，加上甲壳材料较轻，使其重量很低，能凭借膜质翅膀飞行。这也让它们的身体有些透明。

囤积虫因领地性而得名。一旦它们选定某处作为巢穴，就会寻找任何能找到的物体来装饰它，并将这些物体作为巢穴的一部分加以保护。囤积虫独自出现时并不像大型蜂巢中那样危险。不过，如果不去打扰它们，囤积虫意外地中立，几乎不构成威胁。我们喜欢这些愚蠢的抱抱虫！！ - 这是一条来自不屈的 Sigurd 的注释
entries.note: TerminalNode HoardingBugFile 第五批图鉴收尾全文精翻。

entries.source:
HYGRODERE

Sigurd's danger level: 0%, if you're faster than a snail!

Scientific name: Hygrodere 
A eukaryotic organism classified within the paraphyletic group Protista. With the incredible speed of reproduction, these small organisms can multiply to millions. Hygrodere rarely split apart, instead choosing to form large, viscous masses which can take up large amounts of space and become a danger to deal with, requiring large tools or lures to relocate.

Hygrodere are drawn to heat and oxygen and can detect it from seemingly anywhere. There's almost nothing organic they can't convert to their own body mass. Nothing has been found to poison them. Constantly replacing themselves, they can persist for hundreds of thousands of years. If you ever find yourself cornered, find a tall object to stand on top of; hygroderes have trouble climbing. they have great taste! cause I made a friend with one somehow,, and we think it was my music.
entries.target:
黏液怪

西格德危险等级：0%，如果你比蜗牛快的话！

学名：Hygrodere
一种被归类于并系群原生生物的真核生物。凭借惊人的繁殖速度，这些小型生物可以增殖到数百万。黏液怪很少分裂开来，而是选择形成大型黏稠团块，占据大量空间并成为处理时的危险，需要大型工具或诱饵才能迁移。

黏液怪会被热量和氧气吸引，并似乎能从任何地方探测到它们。几乎没有任何有机物是它们无法转化为自身生物质的。尚未发现能毒死它们的物质。通过不断替换自身，它们能持续存在数十万年。如果你发现自己被逼入角落，找一个高物体站上去；黏液怪不擅长攀爬。它们品味很好！因为我不知怎么和其中一个交了朋友，我们觉得是因为我的音乐。

entries.source:
Roaming Locusts

Sigurd's danger level: 0%

Scientific name: Anacridium-vega

Known as roaming locusts, this is a species of grasshopper. Unlike some species which are more prone to jump or fly, roaming locusts are almost never grounded and stay close together even when in smaller numbers. They will quickly disperse when a predator disrupts them but are highly attracted to light.
entries.target:
漫游蝗虫

西格德危险等级：0%

学名：Anacridium-vega

这种草蜢物种被称为漫游蝗虫。不同于一些更倾向于跳跃或飞行的物种，漫游蝗虫几乎从不落地，即使数量较少也会紧密聚集。当捕食者扰乱它们时，它们会迅速四散，但对光高度敏感并会被其吸引。

entries.source:
The inverse teleporter is a modified teleporter which will teleport you to a random position outside the ship. All your items will be dropped at the teleporter before transport. The inverse teleporter can be used by everyone at once and has a 3.5 minute cooldown.

DISCLAIMER: The inverse teleporter can only transport you out, not in, and you may become trapped. The Company is not responsible for injury or replacement of heads and limbs induced by quantum entanglement and bad luck.
